ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference For Guardian Ad Litem To Report On All Actions Taken To Implement The Terms Of The Settlement Agreement And The Results Of Those Actions
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-08-291426 The Hope Trust; PTR-09-292385 The DAF Trust; PTR-09-292386 The Flynn Trust.

Appearances: John Hartog (Guardian Ad Litem) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Hartog reported that World Wrap has been disposed of (transaction consumated) to the Former CFO (Mr. Richardson) of Flynn Industries, that the letter of intent to purchase the parking lot has been withdrawn, that the parking lot is still listed but that there is no interest, and that the parking lot provides a source of income of $4,100.00 per month. Mr. Hartog also stated that the amendments to the Trust have been prepared and reviewed by all Parties, that he expects a petition to filed within the next two or three weeks, and that he will petition for discharge and request fees. Mr. Hartog also spoke of the relationship of Alex and Mali, and the Burger King sales. The status hearings were continued to December 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of a Petition for Modification of Trust and a Petition for Discharge of the Guardian Ad Litem.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-08-2012 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shaver for Donald Alexander Flynn. John Hartog Guardian Ad Litem appeared by telephone for Alex Flynn. Andrew Zabronsky and Philip Feldman for Robert Podesta and Paul Downey (Trustees). Dana Dunwoody appeared by telephone for Mali Flynn. Keith Baldwin appeared by telephone for Flynn Industries.

Counsel addressed the Court's concerns with regard to the true impact the settlement agreement will have on Alex and Mali's financial interests (what is Alex getting, what is Mali getting, what is Alex giving up and what is Mali giving up), the Court being asked to approve transactions that involve corporate and LLC governance, the provision that no one will bring an action against the Guardian Ad Litem as Director of FII (Mr. Hartog playing two rolls), the petition for approval only being brought in the DAF Trust and not in the Flynn Trust and the Hope Trust, etc. Counsel stated that the Corporation will accept fees for the Guardian Ad Litem as a corporate expense. Two seperate petitions for approval of the settlement agreement will be filed. One in the Flynn Trust and one in the Hope Trust. Counsel stated that all Parties waive notice on said petitions. The matters were taken under submission for the Court to go back and re-read the settlement agreement and look at the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

Petition For Allowance Of Fees Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-09-292386 (The Irrevocable Trust Agreement Establishing the Donald Alexander Flynn Trust).

Appearances: John Hartog (Guardian ad Litem) for Alex Flynn. Elliott Steinberg for Mohammad Walizadeh. Andrew Zabronsky for Robert Podesta and Paul Downey. Dana Dunwoody appeared by Court Call for Molly Flynn and Flynn LLP.

Mr. Hartog stated that there was an oversight by the parties, that he has no objections to the stipulations, and that no one has asked him to sign the stipulations. The Court granted the two Steinberg petitions (subject to receipt of Mr. Hartog's signature page). The Court stated that Mr. Hartog could submit a signature page and that it would then sign the orders. The Court then addressed Mr. Hartog's petition and granted his petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.

Joint Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 292386 The Donald Alexander Flynn Trust.

Appearances: Shirley Kovar and Randolph Godshall for Mali Flynn. John Hartog Guardian Ad Litem for Alex Flynn. Andrew Zabronsky for Paul Downey and Robert Podesta.

Mr. Hartog stated that he filed a declaration this morning with the attached valuation report of the Flynn Olympic Restaurants and that he provided copies to opposing Counsel. The Court stated that it had a lot of concerns about the settlement agreement, that there are issues beginning with a conflict in that the current Trustees of each of the two Trusts are the potential buyers of the Trust asset which is income producing, that the Court is being asked to approve a settlement agreement by two of the three Parties to the mediation, that the settlement is really to approve the sale of the asset and that the language in the release and indemnity protect the Trustees without the Minor ever having the opportunity to come back and alledge anything against anyone. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to approval of the settlement agreement, the sale of the Trust asset, valuation of the asset, the sale amount, the Co-Trustees purchasing the asset, conflict of interest, release of the Trustees and the Guardian Ad Litem, resignation of the Co-Trustees, a neutral Trustee being appointed, the Decedent's intentions, the cost of litigation to Alex, etc. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Ex Parte Application For An Order Shortening Time
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Appointment Of Guardian Ad Litem-Probate
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case numbers 291426 The Hope Trust and 292386 The Flynn Trust.

Appearances: Shirley Covar for Mali Flynn. Andrew Zabronsky for Robert Podesta and Robert Downey. David Baer for the Guardian Ad Litem.

The Court stated that the accounting petition on the Hope Trust was put on hold due to the pending appeal and continued it to September 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. In addition, the Court stated that it was prepared to sign the stipulated order appointing Mr. Hartog as the Guardian Ad Litem, that the accounting petition on the DAF Trust filed by Mali Flynn was taken off calendar on August 19, 2009 and therefore, the petition to do with the appointment was also taken off calendar, that it was waiting for the dismissal and that it would not sign any orders appointing Mr. Hartog until it received resignation by Mr. Steinberg in the Flynn and the DAF matters. Ms. Kovar stated that she would file the dismissal on the accounting petition but that she gave no authority to take the appointment of Guardian Ad Litem off calendar. The Court also directed Mr. Baer to submit separate orders for the two separate petitions by the end of the week. The 12th cause of action in the Flynn matter was overruled with 10 days leave to respond and the accounting petition was continued to April 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the amended account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.
